# OpenClaw x Discord Server Integration Guide. Architecture diagram of OpenClaw integrated with Discord server. After embedding an OpenClaw AI agent into a Discord server, your community gains the following capabilities:. Compared to Telegram's personal remote control scenario or Slack's enterprise workflow scenario, the Discord x OpenClaw integration is best suited for **community management, open-source project support, and large-scale multi-user interaction**. Step 1: Create a Discord Bot and Obtain Token. This is the foundation of the entire **OpenClaw Discord setup** process -- creating an Application and Bot on the Discord Developer Portal and obtaining the necessary authentication Token. ### 3.1 Create a Discord Application. ### 3.2 Create Bot and Obtain Token. **Important security reminder:** The Bot Token is equivalent to full access credentials for the Bot -- anyone who has it can control your Bot. Never expose the Token in public places (GitHub, Discord channels, screenshots). After obtaining the Discord Bot Token, the next step is to write it into OpenClaw's configuration so the Gateway can connect to Discord as your Bot.

# OpenClaw cron jobs: Scheduling AI Agents for Automated Tasks — Product Page 2025. Comprehensive product guide for OpenClaw cron jobs: features, architecture, integrations, pricing, security, and best practices to schedule AI agent workflows reliably in 2025. OpenClaw Cron Jobs: Reliable Scheduling for AI Agent Tasks. Reliably Schedule AI Agent Tasks with OpenClaw Cron Jobs – Achieve 99.99% Uptime and Slash Ops Overhead by 50%. OpenClaw cron jobs enable precise, timezone-aware scheduling of AI agents, ensuring reliable execution across distributed environments while minimizing manual interventions (24 words). OpenClaw cron jobs provide a managed scheduling service designed specifically for automating AI agent workflows, enabling teams to orchestrate complex tasks with precision and reliability without managing underlying infrastructure. Unlike traditional cron or Kubernetes CronJob, which require manual scaling and lack built-in AI-specific features, OpenClaw offers serverless execution with retries, idempotency support, and timezone-aware scheduling, making automated agent orchestration seamless for AI/ML teams facing irregular workloads and failure-prone environments.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Jobs: Scheduling, Timing & Payload Options
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

Cron jobs in OpenClaw let you schedule tasks that execute at specific times or intervals without manual intervention. Unlike heartbeats which follow a fixed checklist, cron jobs are individual scheduled tasks with their own timing, payload, and execution context. This lesson covers how to create cron jobs and the different timing and payload options available. * **openclaw cron add**: The CLI command to create a new scheduled job. These two jobs have different schedules and payloads, but both are managed through the same cron system with simple CLI commands. Creating a cron job uses the `openclaw cron add` command with one of three timing options. For schedules that do not fit a simple interval, use standard cron expression syntax:. If you forget to use --every or --cron for recurring tasks, the job will execute once and never run again. * `openclaw cron add` creates scheduled jobs with three timing options: --at (one-time), --every (interval), --cron (expression).

title: 🦞 How to Set Up OpenClaw Cron Jobs (With Examples You Can Copy)
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# How to Set Up OpenClaw Cron Jobs (With Examples You Can Copy). Set up OpenClaw cron jobs with openclaw cron add. Create a cron job: tell your agent "remind me every morning at 9am" or use `openclaw cron add`. Jobs run in isolated sessions by default (fresh context, recommended) or the main session. # Cron schedule (recurring at specific times)# Cron schedule (recurring at specific times) openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Job Name" \ --name  "Job Name"  \ --cron "0 7 * * *" \ --cron "0 7 * * *"  \ --tz "America/Los_Angeles" \ --tz "America/Los_Angeles"  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "What the agent should do" \ --message  "What the agent should do"  \ --announce --announce  # Interval schedule (recurring at fixed intervals)# Interval schedule (recurring at fixed intervals) openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Backup" \ --name  "Backup"  \ --every 3600000 \ --every  3600000  \ --session isolated \ --session  isolated  \ --message "Run git backup" --message  "Run git backup"  # One-time schedule# One-time schedule openclaw cron add \ openclaw  cron  add  \ --name "Reminder" \ --name  "Reminder"  \ --at "2026-03-15T16:00:00Z" \ --at "2026-03-15T16:00:00Z"  \ --session main \ --session  main  \ --system-event "Check the deployment" \ --system-event  "Check the deployment"  \ --delete-after-run --delete-after-run.

title: 🦞 OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide | MI - 超智諮詢
content:
OpenClaw 技术干货

# OpenClaw Cron Scheduled Tasks Guide. Diagram of OpenClaw Cron scheduled task automation execution. What Is OpenClaw's Cron? If you're familiar with Linux's `crontab`, OpenClaw's Cron is conceptually similar -- it automatically executes tasks at specified times. But the key difference is: traditional crontab executes fixed Shell commands, while OpenClaw's Cron executes **natural language instructions**, with the AI agent autonomously deciding how to complete them. `"Every day at 9 AM, check server status and send a summary to Telegram"`. The latter doesn't require you to write scripts in advance -- the agent will decide which check commands to execute, how to organize the results, and how to format the report on its own. `openclaw cron add --schedule "0 9 * * *" --message "Check the server's CPU and memory usage. If a Cron task's execution time exceeds this value, the Gateway will forcibly terminate the connection, causing the task to be truncated -- the agent may have completed 80% of the work, but cannot return results due to the timeout.

# OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs - OpenClaw cron scheduler guide with main and isolated runs. That’s what the built-in cron scheduler is for. It runs inside the Gateway process so you don’t need a separate job runner and it can deliver results straight back to Telegram WhatsApp Slack Discord and more. This isn’t just “run a script at 7 AM.” It’s “wake the agent run a prompt or drop a system event into the main session then deliver output where I will actually see it.”. OpenClaw cron is a scheduler that lives inside the Gateway. You can send the same job output to a private Telegram chat and also to a Slack channel if you want. Another one is a weekly review that runs in a clean isolated session and only posts the final result back to you.
